findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Dynamic class loading via ServiceProvider or reflection with external input
 * @description Class.forName() with potentially user-controlled strings
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/class-forname-rocketmq
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("forName") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Class") and
  not mc.getArgument(0) instanceof StringLiteral
select mc, "Class.forName() called with non-literal argument in: " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName()
findings.hypothesis: Class.forName() called with non-literal (potentially tainted) class names

message.text: Class.forName() called with non-literal argument in: org.apache.rocketmq.store.plugin.MessageStoreFactory.build
Class.forName() called with non-literal argument in: org.apache.rocketmq.tieredstore.container.TieredFileQueue.TieredFileQueue
Class.forName() called with non-literal argument in: org.apache.rocketmq.tieredstore.util.TieredStoreUtil.getMetadataStore

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Fastjson deserialization with autoType
 * @description Fastjson parseObject/parse calls that may enable arbitrary class instantiation
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/fastjson-deserialization-rocketmq
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("parseObject") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("parse") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("parseArray")
  ) and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.alibaba.fastjson", "JSON") and
  mc.getNumArgument() >= 1
select mc, "Fastjson parse/parseObject call that may use autoType deserialization in: " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName()
findings.hypothesis: Fastjson deserialization that may enable arbitrary class instantiation via autoType

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name RocketMQ ServiceProvider dynamic class loading from config
 * @description ServiceProvider loads classes from config that may be user-controlled
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-serviceprovider-classload
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

// Find the ServiceProvider class and how it loads classes
from MethodCall mc, Method m
where
  mc.getMethod() = m and
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.common.utils", "ServiceProvider") and
  (m.hasName("load") or m.hasName("loadClass") or m.hasName("loadService"))
select mc, "ServiceProvider dynamic class loading in: " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName()
findings.hypothesis: ServiceProvider class loads plugin classes that may be influenced by external config

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Fastjson used in remoting protocol decode - network taint confirmed path
 * @description RemotingSerializable.decode (fastjson parseObject with type param) called with 
 *              network-received bytes and used to deserialize broker body payloads
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-fastjson-remoting-confirmed
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

// Find all callers of RemotingSerializable.decode() in broker request processors
// where the body bytes come directly from the RemotingCommand (network)
from MethodCall decodeCall, MethodCall bodyCall
where
  decodeCall.getMethod().hasName("decode") and
  decodeCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.remoting.protocol", "RemotingSerializable") and
  bodyCall.getEnclosingCallable() = decodeCall.getEnclosingCallable() and
  bodyCall.getMethod().hasName("getBody") and
  decodeCall.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("org.apache.rocketmq.broker%")
select decodeCall, "Broker processor uses RemotingSerializable.decode() (fastjson) on network body in: " + decodeCall.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + decodeCall.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " at line " + decodeCall.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Broker processors use fastjson to deserialize untrusted network body bytes

Broker processor uses RemotingSerializable.decode() (fastjson) on network body in: BrokerOuterAPI.getBrokerMemberGroup at line 253

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name FilterServerManager command execution
 * @description FilterServerManager builds and executes a command using broker config - check if config is network-settable
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-filterserver-cmd
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

// Find all Runtime.exec() and ProcessBuilder calls in the codebase
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("exec") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Runtime")
select mc, "Runtime.exec() call in: " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " - argument: " + mc.getArgument(0)
findings.hypothesis: Runtime.exec() calls that may use broker config values settable via network

message.text: Runtime.exec() call in: org.apache.rocketmq.broker.filtersrv.FilterServerUtil.callShell - argument: cmdArray

findings.hypothesis: Network data flows to fastjson parse/parseObject enabling potential autoType deserialization

Fastjson parse with data from [remote source](1).
Fastjson parse with data from [remote source](2).

 * @description Shows how FilterServerManager builds a shell command from BrokerConfig properties

import java

// Find where FilterServerManager calls callShell and what args it uses
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.broker.filtersrv", "FilterServerManager") or

/**
 * @name FilterServerManager buildStartCommand uses mutable BrokerConfig fields
 * @description Shows how the shell command in FilterServerManager is built from BrokerConfig 
 *              which can be updated via updateBrokerConfig network handler

 *       external/cwe/cwe-094

import java

// Find the buildStartCommand method in FilterServerManager 
from Method m, MethodCall fieldAccess
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.broker.filtersrv", "FilterServerManager") and
  m.hasName("buildStartCommand") and
  fieldAccess.getEnclosingCallable() = m

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name updateBrokerConfig network handler - confirm path from network body to broker config setter
 * @description AdminBrokerProcessor.updateBrokerConfig decodes Properties from network body
 *              and applies via reflection, allowing rocketmqHome and other fields to be modified
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-admin-updateconfig-details
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

// Find all methods in AdminBrokerProcessor that call properties2Object or MixAll string-to-object
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.broker.processor", "AdminBrokerProcessor") and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("properties2Object") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("string2Properties") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getBody") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("string2File")
  )
select mc, "AdminBrokerProcessor config update call: " + mc.getMethod().getName() + " in method: " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: AdminBrokerProcessor.updateBrokerConfig manipulates config via network body bytes

/**
 * @name NameServer DefaultRequestProcessor updateConfig - check for auth bypass
 * @description Check if DefaultRequestProcessor.updateConfig (CVE-2023-37582) 
 *              has proper authentication before applying config changes
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-namesrv-updateconfig-handler
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

// Find the updateConfig method and what it does
from Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.namesrv.processor", "DefaultRequestProcessor") and
  m.hasName("updateConfig") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m
select mc, "DefaultRequestProcessor.updateConfig call: " + mc.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name NameServer updateConfig - check for authentication bypass
 * @description CVE-2023-37582: NameServer updateConfig processes network body without auth check
 *              Verify if isWithoutAuth check exists or if any acl check is present
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-namesrv-updateconfig-auth
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

// Check what handlers are registered in DefaultRequestProcessor and if updateConfig checks auth
from Method m
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.namesrv.processor", "DefaultRequestProcessor") and
  (m.hasName("updateConfig") or m.hasName("processRequest"))
select m, "DefaultRequestProcessor method: " + m.getName() + " has " + m.getNumberOfParameters() + " params, lines: " + m.getBody().getNumStmt()
findings.hypothesis: CVE-2023-37582 - check if NameServer updateConfig has authentication

message.text: DefaultRequestProcessor method: processRequest has 2 params, lines: 2
DefaultRequestProcessor method: updateConfig has 2 params, lines: 7

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name NameServer processRequest dispatch - check if UPDATE_NAMESRV_CONFIG requires auth
 * @description Check whether the NameServer request dispatcher has authentication for config update
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-namesrv-dispatch-auth
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

// Find the switch/dispatch in DefaultRequestProcessor that routes to updateConfig
// Look for whether there's an auth check before routing to updateConfig
from SwitchStmt sw, MethodCall updateCall
where
  sw.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.namesrv.processor", "DefaultRequestProcessor") and
  updateCall.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.namesrv.processor", "DefaultRequestProcessor") and
  updateCall.getMethod().hasName("updateConfig")
select sw, "Switch statement in DefaultRequestProcessor at line " + sw.getLocation().getStartLine() + " dispatches to updateConfig"
findings.hypothesis: NameServer dispatch routing to updateConfig - checking for auth guard presence

message.text: Switch statement in DefaultRequestProcessor at line 90 dispatches to updateConfig

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name UpdateBrokerConfig - getBody call uses network request bytes
 * @description Confirm that AdminBrokerProcessor.updateBrokerConfig directly uses request.getBody()
 *              which comes from the network, and feeds into string2Properties then properties2Object
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-broker-updateconfig-confirm
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

// Check the exact body of updateBrokerConfig method to understand the flow
from Method m, MethodCall bodyCall, MethodCall propsCall
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.broker.processor", "AdminBrokerProcessor") and
  m.hasName("updateBrokerConfig") and
  bodyCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  bodyCall.getMethod().hasName("getBody") and
  propsCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m
select propsCall, "updateBrokerConfig method call: " + propsCall.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + propsCall.getLocation().getStartLine() + " (request.getBody() at line " + bodyCall.getLocation().getStartLine() + ")"
findings.hypothesis: Confirm updateBrokerConfig reads network body and applies to broker config via reflection

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Configuration.update() uses reflection to apply network-controlled properties
 * @description The Configuration.update() method applies Properties to config objects via reflection
 *              This is used by both Broker and NameServer to apply network-received config changes
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-configuration-update-reflection
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

// Inspect what Configuration.update() does - find reflection calls within it
from Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.remoting", "Configuration") and
  m.hasName("update") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m
select mc, "Configuration.update() calls: " + mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Configuration.update() uses reflection to blindly apply network-received properties

message.text: Configuration.update() calls: Lock.lockInterruptibly at line 181
Configuration.update() calls: ReadWriteLock.writeLock at line 181

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Expression language injection via SQL filter expressions in RocketMQ
 * @description SQL92 filter expressions processed by broker - check if user-controlled
 *              filter expressions enable code execution or dangerous evaluation
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rocketmq-sql-filter-injection
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-917
 */

import java

// Find where SQL filter expressions are parsed/compiled from request data
// ConsumerFilterManager compiles filter expressions from subscription data
from MethodCall mc
where
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("compile") and
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.filter", "FilterFactory")
  ) or (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("compile") and
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("org.apache.rocketmq.filter%")
  ) or (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("build") and
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.rocketmq.broker.filter", "ConsumerFilterManager")
  )
select mc, "Filter expression compile/build in: " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: SQL filter expression compilation from user-controlled subscription data

message.text: Filter expression compile/build in: org.apache.rocketmq.broker.filter.ConsumerFilterManager.build at line 94
Filter expression compile/build in: org.apache.rocketmq.broker.filter.ConsumerFilterManager.decode at line 249
Filter expression compile/build in: org.apache.rocketmq.broker.processor.ClientManageProcessor.checkClientConfig at line 206
